skill.name: gemini-research-subagent
skill.description: Delegates large-context code research to Gemini CLI so an agent can analyze broad code areas before implementing changes.
skill.summary: Use Gemini CLI for large-context codebase research, planning, debugging, review, and verification.
skill.author: DasBluEyedDevil

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly detected external command examples, but they are Markdown usage instructions rather than embedded executable code. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary words in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is that the skill instructs agents to invoke Gemini CLI with repository paths, prompts, and diffs, which can expose project context to an external model service.

medium_findings.title: External Gemini CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run ./skills/gemini.agent.wrapper.sh with repository paths, questions, and diffs. This is a legitimate research workflow, but it creates external command execution risk and can disclose code context to Gemini CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated. The risk is medium because the file only provides Markdown instructions, and no wrapper implementation is present in the scanned skill.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Code Context Sharing
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to use Gemini for reading large codebases and verifying diffs. That workflow may send source code, bug details, and implementation context to a third-party model service through Gemini CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill purpose clearly delegates codebase reading to Gemini CLI. Exact network behavior depends on the external CLI and wrapper, which were not included in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static findings for weak cryptographic algorithms correspond to ordinary descriptive text, including the front matter description and the api-designer role label. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm use is present in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive Markdown text, not cryptographic code. The apparent matches are substring-based false positives.

low_findings.title: Markdown Role Flags Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are role names or flag examples in tables, not executable code blocks. They do not execute by themselves, but they document command options for the wrapper.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is a Markdown table of roles and labels. It is still relevant documentation, but it is not executable source code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow invokes a local shell wrapper for Gemini CLI. Users should review the wrapper and avoid sending confidential repository content without approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are visible in Markdown examples. The risk depends on the external wrapper and Gemini CLI behavior.

content.value_statement: Large repositories can exceed the practical context window for direct analysis. This skill routes broad research, planning, debugging, and verification tasks through Gemini CLI before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Provides command patterns for sending large code areas to Gemini CLI.
Defines roles for review, debugging, planning, security, migration, and documentation.
Supports template-based requests for implementation planning, bug fixes, and verification.
Describes a research-first workflow where Gemini analyzes and the primary agent implements.
Specifies a structured response format with summary, files, analysis, and recommendations.
limitations: Requires Gemini CLI and the referenced wrapper script to be installed separately.
Does not include the wrapper implementation in the scanned skill package.
May send repository content or diffs to an external model service.
Does not implement code changes directly; it only guides research delegation.

use_cases.title: Plan a Cross-File Feature
use_cases.description: Ask Gemini to inspect a broad source tree and return an implementation plan before changes begin.

use_cases.title: Trace a Complex Bug
use_cases.description: Use the debugger role to follow a failure across multiple modules and identify likely root causes.

use_cases.title: Review Architecture and Security
use_cases.description: Run a broad review role to surface design risks, security concerns, and files that need attention.

prompt_templates.title: Explore a Code Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini to summarize the main responsibilities of @src/ and identify the files I should read first.

prompt_templates.title: Trace a Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the debugger role to investigate why login returns 401. Include likely causes, affected files, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planner role on @src/ to design a caching layer. Return implementation steps, risks, and files to modify.

prompt_templates.title: Verify a Multi-File Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verify template with the current diff to check whether the new password reset flow is complete and safe.

output_examples.input: Research how to add user profiles to the application.
output_examples.output: A concise plan that names relevant files, summarizes current data flow, and lists implementation steps.

output_examples.input: Debug a login failure that returns 401.
output_examples.output: A root cause analysis with suspected modules, evidence, and concrete checks to confirm the fix.

output_examples.input: Verify a caching change after implementation.
output_examples.output: A review summary covering changed behavior, regression risks, and recommended follow-up tests.

best_practices: Use Gemini for broad reading before making multi-file changes.
Review the exact files and diffs that will be sent before invoking Gemini CLI.
Use the structured output to decide implementation steps, then verify changes locally.
anti_patterns: Do not send private code, secrets, or sensitive diffs without approval.
Do not treat Gemini output as final authority without checking the referenced files.
Do not use this workflow for simple single-file edits that fit local context.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an agent to use Gemini CLI for large-context code research before implementation.

faq.question: Does it write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill says Gemini should read and analyze, while the primary agent handles implementation.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: It requires Gemini CLI and the referenced local wrapper script to be available in the environment.

faq.question: Can it help with security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It defines a security role for broad vulnerability review, but results still need human judgment.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow may send code context or diffs to Gemini CLI and its backing service.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill documents external command use and third-party code analysis. No malicious intent was found.
